title: Quantification of Nuclear Mitochondrial DNA Insertions (NUMTs) Using Fiber-FI
author: Jeevan Labh

steps.title: Harvest cultured cells by scraping into 5 ml ice-cold PBS
steps.instruction: Harvest cultured cells by scraping into 5 ml ice-cold PBS.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 800 × g for...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 800 × g for 5 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Discard the supernatant and resuspend the...
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant and resuspend the pellet in 5 ml Solution A. Solution A: 20 mM HEPES-KOH, pH 7.6; 220 mM mannitol; 70 mM sucrose; 1 mM EDTA; 0.5 mM PMSF; 2 mg/ml BSA

steps.title: Homogenize cells using a glass homogenizer...
steps.instruction: Homogenize cells using a glass homogenizer with 20–30 gentle strokes on ice.

steps.title: Centrifuge homogenate at 800 × g...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge homogenate at 800 × g for 5 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Transfer the supernatant to a new tube
steps.instruction: Transfer the supernatant to a new tube.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 10,000 × g for...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 10,000 × g for 10 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Discard the supernatant carefully
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant carefully.

steps.title: Resuspend the mitochondrial pellet in Solution B. Solution B
steps.instruction: Resuspend the mitochondrial pellet in Solution B. Solution B: 20 mM Hepes-KOH pH 7.6; 220 mM mannitol; 70 mM sucrose; 1 mM EDTA; 0.5 mM PMSF

steps.title: Perform a clarifying centrifugation at 800...
steps.instruction: Perform a clarifying centrifugation at 800 × g for 5 min.

steps.title: Transfer the supernatant and centrifuge again...
steps.instruction: Transfer the supernatant and centrifuge again at 10,000 × g for 10–20 min.

steps.title: Resuspend the final mitochondrial pellet in sucrose buffer
steps.instruction: Resuspend the final mitochondrial pellet in sucrose buffer.

steps.title: Store on ice until slide preparation
steps.instruction: Store on ice until slide preparation.

steps.title: Resuspend harvested cells in nuclear isolation...
steps.instruction: Resuspend harvested cells in nuclear isolation lysis buffer containing 10% IGEPAL. Nuclear Lysis Buffer: 10 mM HEPES, pH 7.9; 10 mM KCl; 0.1 mM EDTA

steps.title: Incubate for 10 min at room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate for 10 min at room temperature.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 15,000 × g for 3 min
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 15,000 × g for 3 min.

steps.title: Carefully discard the supernatant
steps.instruction: Carefully discard the supernatant.

steps.title: Repeat the lysis procedure 2–3 additional...
steps.instruction: Repeat the lysis procedure 2–3 additional times to minimize mitochondrial contamination.

steps.title: Resuspend purified nuclei in PBS
steps.instruction: Resuspend purified nuclei in PBS.

steps.title: Lyse the purified nuclei in RIPA Buffer. RIPA Buffer
steps.instruction: Lyse the purified nuclei in RIPA Buffer. RIPA Buffer: Tris-HCl, 50 mM; Sodium Chloride, 150 mM; Triton X-100, 1%; Sodium deoxycholate, 0.5%; Sodium lauryl sulphate, 0.1%

steps.title: High powered sonication (3 sets of...
steps.instruction: High powered sonication (3 sets of 10 seconds sonication at 20% amplitude) is required to break the nuclear envelope and shear the DNA.

steps.title: BCA Assay is used to quantify the total Protein concentration
steps.instruction: BCA Assay is used to quantify the total Protein concentration.

steps.title: Western Blotting Parameters
steps.instruction: Western Blotting Parameters: 4-20% Gradient SDS-PAGE on 0.2 μm PVDF membrane. Use 5% non-fat milk in TBST for 1 hour for blocking. Primary Antibody: Rabbit Polyclonal or Mouse Monoclonal Anti-COXII, 1:1000 in 5% BSA, overnight at 4℃. Secondary Antibody: HRP-conjugated secondary; 1:5000 in 5% non-fat milk.

steps.title: Strong band in mitochondrial lane, and...
steps.instruction: Strong band in mitochondrial lane, and no band in nuclear lane signifies purity. Use twice the protein load in nuclear lane for more accurate results.

steps.title: Probes were prepared using PCR primers...
steps.instruction: Probes were prepared using PCR primers as developed by Singh et al., 2018.

steps.title: Verify PCR products by agarose gel electrophoresis
steps.instruction: Verify PCR products by agarose gel electrophoresis.

steps.title: Purify amplified products using Centri-Sep spin...
steps.instruction: Purify amplified products using Centri-Sep spin columns according to the manufacturer’s instructions.

steps.title: Prepare the nick translation reaction mixture
steps.instruction: Prepare the nick translation reaction mixture: 5 μl 0.2 mM dATP/dCTP/dGTP mixture; 1 μl labeled nucleotide (biotin-16-dUTP, digoxigenin-11-dUTP, or DNP-11-dUTP); 1 μg probe DNA; 5 μl DNase I/DNA polymerase mixture; Distilled water to 50 μl total volume

steps.title: Mix gently and centrifuge briefly
steps.instruction: Mix gently and centrifuge briefly.

steps.title: Incubate at 15 °C for 120 min
steps.instruction: Incubate at 15 °C for 120 min.

steps.title: Heat inactivate at 65 °C for 10 min
steps.instruction: Heat inactivate at 65 °C for 10 min.

steps.title: Allow tubes to cool at room temperature for 15 min
steps.instruction: Allow tubes to cool at room temperature for 15 min.

steps.title: Add 6
steps.instruction: Add 6.25 μl 4 M ammonium acetate and 5 μl 100% ethanol.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 12,000 rpm for 5 min
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 12,000 rpm for 5 min.

steps.title: Remove supernatant carefully
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant carefully.

steps.title: Resuspend labeled probe pellet in 20 μl 100% formamide
steps.instruction: Resuspend labeled probe pellet in 20 μl 100% formamide.

steps.title: Store at −20 °C until use
steps.instruction: Store at −20 °C until use.

steps.title: Apply 5 μl resuspended mitochondrial DNA...
steps.instruction: Apply 5 μl resuspended mitochondrial DNA to the center of a Poly-L-Lysine-coated slide.

steps.title: Add 15 μl Slide Preparation Lysis Buffer 1
steps.instruction: Add 15 μl Slide Preparation Lysis Buffer 1. (2% Sarkosyl; 0.25% SDS; 50 mM Tris-HCl, pH 7.4; 50 mM EDTA, pH 8.0; 0.5% Triton X-100)

steps.title: Incubate slides at 37 °C for 10 min
steps.instruction: Incubate slides at 37 °C for 10 min.

steps.title: Gently place a 22 × 22...
steps.instruction: Gently place a 22 × 22 mm coverslip over the suspension.

steps.title: Fix fibers at 60 °C for 30 min
steps.instruction: Fix fibers at 60 °C for 30 min.

steps.title: Remove coverslips using a 3:1 ethanol:glacial acetic acid solution
steps.instruction: Remove coverslips using a 3:1 ethanol:glacial acetic acid solution.

steps.title: Dry slides at 60 °C for 15 min
steps.instruction: Dry slides at 60 °C for 15 min.

steps.title: Deposit 2 μl nuclei suspension on...
steps.instruction: Deposit 2 μl nuclei suspension on one end of a Poly-L-Lysine slide.

steps.title: Allow the suspension to dry briefly
steps.instruction: Allow the suspension to dry briefly.

steps.title: Add 10 μl Slide Preparation Lysis Buffer 2
steps.instruction: Add 10 μl Slide Preparation Lysis Buffer 2. (0.5% SDS; 5 mM EDTA; 100 mM Tris-HCl, pH 7.0)

steps.title: Incubate at room temperature for 4 min
steps.instruction: Incubate at room temperature for 4 min.

steps.title: Extend DNA fibers using a coverslip
steps.instruction: Extend DNA fibers using a coverslip.

steps.title: Air-dry slides completely
steps.instruction: Air-dry slides completely.

steps.title: Fix slides in 3:1 ethanol:glacial acetic acid for 2 min
steps.instruction: Fix slides in 3:1 ethanol:glacial acetic acid for 2 min.

steps.title: Heat slides at 60 °C for 30 min
steps.instruction: Heat slides at 60 °C for 30 min.

steps.title: Prepare hybridization mixture containing labeled probes
steps.instruction: Prepare hybridization mixture containing labeled probes. (50 ng DNA probe; 50% formamide; 10% dextran sulfate; 10 mM EDTA; 3 M NaCl; 5 μg salmon sperm DNA)

steps.title: Apply probe mixture to prepared DNA fibers
steps.instruction: Apply probe mixture to prepared DNA fibers.

steps.title: Cover with a coverslip and seal edges
steps.instruction: Cover with a coverslip and seal edges.

steps.title: Incubate slides overnight at 37 °C in a humidified chamber
steps.instruction: Incubate slides overnight at 37 °C in a humidified chamber.

steps.title: Wash slides twice in 2× SSC...
steps.instruction: Wash slides twice in 2× SSC for 5 min each at room temperature.

steps.title: Wash in 50% formamide/2× SSC at...
steps.instruction: Wash in 50% formamide/2× SSC at 42 °C for 10 min.

steps.title: Wash in 2× SSC at 42 °C for 10 min
steps.instruction: Wash in 2× SSC at 42 °C for 10 min.

steps.title: Air-dry slides protected from light
steps.instruction: Air-dry slides protected from light.

steps.title: For Digoxigenin labeled probes Incubate slides...
steps.instruction: For Digoxigenin labeled probes Incubate slides with mouse anti-digoxigenin antibody (1:200 dilution). Wash with PBS. Incubate with rabbit anti-mouse Alexa Fluor 568 antibody (1:200 dilution).

steps.title: Biotin-labeled probes
steps.instruction: Biotin-labeled probes: Incubate slides with goat anti-biotin antibody conjugated with avidin (1:200 dilution). Wash with PBS. Incubate with streptavidin-Alexa Fluor 488 (1:200 dilution).

steps.title: DNP-Labeled Probes
steps.instruction: DNP-Labeled Probes: Incubate slides with rabbit anti-DNP antibody. Wash with PBS. Incubate with chicken anti-rabbit Alexa Fluor 647 antibody. Wash slides thoroughly and mount using antifade mounting medium.

steps.title: Acquire Fiber-FISH images using a Zeiss...
steps.instruction: Acquire Fiber-FISH images using a Zeiss Axioplan 2 fluorescence microscope equipped with a CoolSNAP HQ2 CCD camera.

steps.title: Capture images using AxioVision 4
steps.instruction: Capture images using AxioVision 4.8 software.

steps.title: Process image contrast uniformly using Adobe Photoshop CS5
steps.instruction: Process image contrast uniformly using Adobe Photoshop CS5.

steps.title: Perform signal measurements and fiber length analysis using ImageJ software
steps.instruction: Perform signal measurements and fiber length analysis using ImageJ software.

steps.title: Quantify NUMT events by identifying mtDNA...
steps.instruction: Quantify NUMT events by identifying mtDNA probe signals localized within nuclear DNA fibers.

steps.title: Count total nuclear DNA fibers analyzed
steps.instruction: Count total nuclear DNA fibers analyzed.

steps.title: Count fibers containing mtDNA integration signals
steps.instruction: Count fibers containing mtDNA integration signals.

steps.title: Calculate NUMT frequency as
steps.instruction: Calculate NUMT frequency as: NUMT Frequency = (Number of fibers containing mtDNA signals / Total nuclear fibers analyzed) × 100

steps.title: Measure insertion signal length where applicable
steps.instruction: Measure insertion signal length where applicable.
